title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
description: A comedic science fiction series following Arthur Dent as he travels through space after Earth is demolished. Full of absurd humor, philosophical questions, and satirical takes on life, the universe, and everything.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: Set in a distant future amidst a feudal interstellar society, it follows Paul Atreides as his family accepts control of the desert planet Arrakis. A deep exploration of politics, religion, ecology, and human potential.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society ruled by Big Brother. Winston Smith secretly rebels against the oppressive regime. A chilling warning about surveillance, propaganda, and loss of individual freedom.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: The tale of Kvothe, a legendary wizard and hero, telling his own story from his humble beginnings to his mythical status. Rich world-building, a unique magic system, and beautiful prose.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping narrative of humanity's creation and evolution, exploring how Homo sapiens came to dominate Earth. Covers cognitive revolution, agricultural revolution, and the rise of empires, money, and religion.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A young Andalusian shepherd travels to Egypt in search of treasure. Along the journey he meets mentors, falls in love, and learns the true meaning of Personal Legend and following one's dreams.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: Practical strategies for forming good habits, breaking bad ones, and mastering the tiny behaviors that lead to remarkable results. Based on proven scientific research and real-world examples.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: David Thomas, Andrew Hunt
description: Essential reading for software developers. Covers best practices, tips for working smarter, career advice, and fundamental programming concepts that stand the test of time.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: A memoir about a woman who grows up in a survivalist family in rural Idaho, never attending school, yet eventually earns a PhD from Cambridge University. A story of self-invention and the power of education.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: Between life and death there is a library. Nora Seed finds herself in the Midnight Library where she can try out different versions of her life before making her final decision. A beautiful meditation on regret and possibility.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship. Teaches how to write code that is readable, maintainable, and elegant. Full of practical examples and case studies in Java.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: Ryland Grace wakes up alone on a spacecraft with no memory of how he got there. He soon discovers he's on a mission to save Earth from an extinction-level threat. Filled with clever science and an unforgettable alien encounter.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: Explores the two systems that drive the way we think: System 1 (fast, intuitive) and System 2 (slow, deliberate). Reveals cognitive biases and how they affect our decisions.

title: The Lord of the Rings
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: The epic tale of the Fellowship's quest to destroy the One Ring and defeat the Dark Lord Sauron. A masterpiece of world-building with rich languages, history, and lore.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A futuristic society where people are genetically engineered, conditioned, and kept happy through a drug called soma. Explores themes of freedom, individuality, and what it means to be human.

title: The Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
description: A methodology for developing businesses and products that aims to shorten product development cycles. Advocates for building minimum viable products and iterating based on customer feedback.

title: Norwegian Wood
author: Haruki Murakami
description: A nostalgic story of loss and sexuality set in Tokyo during the late 1960s. Toru Watanabe looks back on his days as a student, his friendship with Kizuki, and his love for two very different women.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: Explores the major considerations for designing data systems including reliability, scalability, and maintainability. Covers databases, distributed systems, stream processing, and more.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: Set in the Jazz Age on Long Island, the novel depicts narrator Nick Carraway's interactions with the mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and Gatsby's obsession with his former lover Daisy Buchanan.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
description: Argues that the ability to focus without distraction on cognitively demanding tasks is becoming increasingly rare and valuable. Provides a training regimen for cultivating a deep work ethic.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
description: The story of washed-up computer hacker Henry Case hired for one last job, which brings him into contact with a mysterious AI. The novel that defined cyberpunk and introduced the concept of cyberspace.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
description: A psychiatrist and Holocaust survivor describes his psychotherapeutic method of finding meaning in all forms of existence, even in suffering. One of the most influential books of the 20th century.

title: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
description: An astronaut becomes stranded on Mars after his team assumes him dead and evacuates during a storm. He must use his wit and botanical knowledge to survive until a rescue can arrive.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
description: Notes on startups and how to build the future. Thiel argues that true innovation means going from zero to one — creating something genuinely new rather than copying what already exists.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: Raskolnikov, a destitute ex-student in Saint Petersburg, commits a murder and then grapples with the moral and psychological consequences. A profound exploration of guilt, redemption, and the human psyche.

title: Danse Macabre
author: Stephen King
description: A highly rated horror work by Stephen King. Published in 1980, this book explores themes of art, space.

title: It
author: Stephen King
description: A highly rated horror work by Stephen King. Published in 1986, this book explores themes of science.

title: The Shining
author: Stephen King
description: A highly rated horror work by Stephen King. Published in 1977, this book explores themes of general fiction, literature.

title: Misery
author: Stephen King
description: A highly rated horror work by Stephen King. Published in 1978, this book explores themes of general fiction, literature.

title: Carrie
author: Stephen King
description: A highly rated horror work by Stephen King. Published in 1974, this book explores themes of general fiction, literature.

title: A Guide to the Good Life
author: William B. Irvine
description: A highly rated philosophy work by William B. Irvine. Published in 2009, this book explores themes of general fiction, literature.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
description: A highly rated philosophy work by Marcus Aurelius. Published in 1626, this book explores themes of philosophy, ethics.
